name: additional-context-menus
description: Supercharge your Node.js development with intelligent right-click menus. Extract functions, generate enums/env/cron, and refactor React/Angular/Express/Next.js code in seconds. Features AST-based detection, smart imports, and 60KB optimized bundle.

screenshots.label: Extract functions with intelligent import handling
Smart file discovery with framework-aware filtering

walkthroughs.title: Get Started with Additional Context Menus
walkthroughs.description: Discover how to supercharge your Node.js development workflow with intelligent right-click menus.

steps.title: Copy a Function
steps.description:
Place your cursor inside any function, then right-click and choose **Copy Function** to copy it to your clipboard.

[Open a TypeScript file](command:workbench.action.files.openFile)

steps.title: Copy Function to File
steps.description:
Place your cursor inside a function, right-click and choose **Copy Function to File** to copy it directly into another file.

[Try it now](command:additionalContextMenus.copyFunctionToFile)

media.altText: Demonstration of Copy Function to File feature

steps.title: Move Function to File
steps.description:
Place your cursor inside a function, right-click and choose **Move Function to File** to move it to another file, removing it from the source.

[Try it now](command:additionalContextMenus.moveFunctionToFile)

media.altText: Demonstration of Move Function to File feature

steps.title: Copy Selection to File
steps.description:
Select any code block, right-click, and choose **Copy Selection to File** to copy it into another file in your project.

[Try it now](command:additionalContextMenus.copySelectionToFile)

media.altText: Demonstration of Copy Selection to File feature

steps.title: Move Selection to File
steps.description:
Select any code block, right-click, and choose **Move Selection to File** to move it to another file, removing it from the source.

[Try it now](command:additionalContextMenus.moveSelectionToFile)

media.altText: Demonstration of Move Selection to File feature

steps.title: Save All Files
steps.description:
Right-click anywhere in the editor and choose **Save All** to save every open file at once.

[Try it now](command:additionalContextMenus.saveAll)

steps.title: Open in Terminal
steps.description:
Right-click any file and choose **Open in Terminal** to instantly open a terminal at that location.

[Configure terminal settings](command:workbench.action.openSettings?%5B%22additionalContextMenus.terminal%22%5D)

media.altText: Demonstration of Open in Terminal feature

steps.title: Rename File to Convention
steps.description:
Right-click any file or folder in the Explorer and choose **Rename File to Convention** to rename to kebab-case, camelCase, or PascalCase.

[Try it now](command:additionalContextMenus.renameFileConvention)

media.altText: Demonstration of Rename File to Convention feature

additionalContextMenus.enabled.description: Enable or disable Additional Context Menus extension
additionalContextMenus.enabled.markdownDescription:
Enable or disable the **Additional Context Menus** extension.

**Example:** Set to `false` to temporarily hide all context menu items without uninstalling the extension.

additionalContextMenus.autoDetectProject.description: Automatically detect frameworks for informational purposes
additionalContextMenus.autoDetectProject.markdownDescription:
Automatically detect Node.js frameworks (React, Angular, Express, Next.js) in the workspace for informational purposes.

**Example:** When `true`, the extension identifies your framework and tailors context menu labels accordingly.

additionalContextMenus.supportedExtensio.description: File extensions where context menus will be shown
additionalContextMenus.supportedExtensio.markdownDescription:
List of file extensions for which context menu items are displayed.

**Default:** `[".ts", ".tsx", ".js", ".jsx"]`

**Example:** Add `".mts"` or `".cts"` to support additional TypeScript module formats.

additionalContextMenus.copyCode.insertio.description: Where to insert copied code in target file
additionalContextMenus.copyCode.insertio.markdownDescription:
Controls where copied code is inserted in the target file.

- `smart` *(default)* — inserts after the last import statement, before the first export, or at the end
- `end` — always appends to the bottom of the file
- `beginning` — always inserts at the top of the file

**Example:** Use `"end"` if you prefer all pasted code to appear at the bottom of the target file.

additionalContextMenus.copyCode.handleIm.description: How to handle import statements when copying code
additionalContextMenus.copyCode.handleIm.markdownDescription:
Determines how import statements are handled when copying code to a target file.

- `merge` *(default)* — combines new imports with existing ones, avoiding duplicates
- `duplicate` — copies imports as-is, even if they already exist in the target
- `skip` — omits import statements entirely from the copied code

**Example:** Use `"skip"` when copying utility functions that share imports already present in the target file.

additionalContextMenus.copyCode.preserve.markdownDescription:
When `true`, JSDoc and inline comments are included when copying or moving code.

**Example:** Set to `false` to strip all comments from copied code for a cleaner paste.

additionalContextMenus.saveAll.showNotif.description: Show notification after saving all files
additionalContextMenus.saveAll.showNotif.markdownDescription:
When `true`, a notification is shown in the bottom-right corner after the **Save All** command completes.

**Example:** Set to `false` for a silent save-all experience.

additionalContextMenus.saveAll.skipReadO.description: Skip read-only files when saving all
additionalContextMenus.saveAll.skipReadO.markdownDescription:
When `true`, read-only files are silently skipped during **Save All** instead of triggering an error.

**Example:** Set to `false` if you want to be notified when a read-only file cannot be saved.

additionalContextMenus.terminal.type.description: Type of terminal to open when using 'Open in Terminal' command
additionalContextMenus.terminal.type.markdownDescription:
Selects which terminal is opened by the **Open in Terminal** command.

- `integrated` *(default)* — opens VS Code's built-in terminal panel
- `external` — launches the terminal defined in `#additionalContextMenus.terminal.externalTerminalCommand#`
- `system-default` — uses the OS default terminal application

**Example:** Use `"external"` with `externalTerminalCommand` set to `"wt -d {{directory}}"` to open Windows Terminal.

additionalContextMenus.terminal.external.description: Custom command for external terminal. Use {{directory}} as placeholder for directory path
additionalContextMenus.terminal.external.markdownDescription:
Custom shell command used when `#additionalContextMenus.terminal.type#` is set to `external`.

Use `{{directory}}` as a placeholder for the target directory path.

**Examples:**
- macOS iTerm2: `open -a iTerm {{directory}}`
- Windows Terminal: `wt -d {{directory}}`
- GNOME Terminal: `gnome-terminal --working-directory={{directory}}`

additionalContextMenus.terminal.openBeha.description: Which directory to open in terminal
additionalContextMenus.terminal.openBeha.markdownDescription:
Determines the working directory used when opening a terminal.

- `parent-directory` *(default)* — opens the folder containing the right-clicked file
- `workspace-root` — always opens the workspace root folder
- `current-directory` — opens the directory of the currently active editor

**Example:** Use `"workspace-root"` to always start terminals at the project root regardless of which file you right-clicked.

additionalContextMenus.accessibility.ver.description: Control verbosity of accessibility announcements for screen readers. Minimal: Only errors and critical operations. Normal: All operations (default). Verbose: Detailed progress and contextual information.
additionalContextMenus.accessibility.ver.markdownDescription:
Controls how much information is announced to screen readers during extension operations.

- `minimal` — only errors and critical operations are announced
- `normal` *(default)* — all operations are announced
- `verbose` — detailed progress and contextual information is announced

**Example:** Use `"minimal"` to reduce screen reader noise during rapid copy/paste workflows.
enumDescriptions: Only announce errors and critical operations
Announce detailed progress and contextual information

additionalContextMenus.accessibility.scr.description: Enable enhanced screen reader support with additional announcements and ARIA labels
additionalContextMenus.accessibility.scr.markdownDescription:
When `true`, enables enhanced screen reader support including additional ARIA labels and more descriptive announcements in Quick Pick dialogs.

**Example:** Enable this setting when using NVDA, JAWS, or VoiceOver for a more accessible experience.

additionalContextMenus.accessibility.key.description: Enable enhanced keyboard navigation hints in Quick Pick dialogs
additionalContextMenus.accessibility.key.markdownDescription:
When `true`, Quick Pick dialogs display keyboard shortcut hints to help navigate options without a mouse.

**Example:** Set to `false` if you prefer a cleaner Quick Pick UI without navigation hints.

scripts.rebuild: pnpm run clean && pnpm run build && vsce package
